chunk_summary: This section provides a stepwise approach to calculate energy and protein requirements for Indian children with chronic undernutrition, using ICMR-NIN guidelines and worked examples for stunting with or without wasting.
learning_objectives: Understand the stepwise method for calculating energy and protein requirements in children with chronic undernutrition.
Learn how to use Indian reference values for energy and protein needs based on age and weight.
Differentiate between nutritional prescriptions for stunting with and without wasting.
Apply catch-up nutrition principles to create tailored dietary plans for undernourished children.
key_facts: Baseline energy needs: 83 kcal/kg/day (1–3 years), 74 kcal/kg/day (4–6 years), 67 kcal/kg/day (7–9 years).
Baseline protein needs: 0.97 g/kg/day (1–3 years), 0.87 g/kg/day (4–6 years), 0.92 g/kg/day (7–9 years).
For stunting without wasting, increase energy intake by 10–15% above baseline.
For stunting with low weight-for-age or mild wasting, increase energy intake by 20–30%.
Example: A 2-year-old weighing 10 kg requires 830 kcal/day and 9.7 g/day protein; with a 15% increment, the target is 955 kcal/day.
Severe acute malnutrition rehabilitation targets should not be applied to stunted-only children.
reference_quote: A practical clinic shortcut from ICMR-NIN reference values is energy about 83 kcal/kg/day at 1–3 years, 74 kcal/kg/day at 4–6 years, and 67 kcal/kg/day at 7–9 years.
document_key_topics: Energy and protein requirements for stunted children
